title: sort, uniq, wc & Text Utilities
content:
### sort — Sort Lines

| Option | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `-n` | Numeric sort |
| `-r` | Reverse order |
| `-k` | Sort by column/field |
| `-t` | Field separator |
| `-u` | Unique (same as sort then uniq) |
| `-h` | Human-readable sizes (K, M, G) |

```bash
sort file.txt
sort -n numbers.txt
sort -t: -k3 -n /etc/passwd    # sort by 3rd field (UID)
du -h | sort -rh               # sort by size
```

### uniq — Filter Repeated Lines

Requires sorted input (or use `sort -u`).

```bash
uniq file.txt                  # remove adjacent duplicates
uniq -c file.txt               # count occurrences
uniq -d file.txt               # only show duplicates
uniq -u file.txt               # only show unique lines
```

### wc — Word Count

```bash
wc file.txt                    # lines, words, bytes
wc -l file.txt                 # line count
wc -w file.txt                 # word count
wc -c file.txt                 # byte count
wc -m file.txt                 # character count (multibyte)
```

### cut — Extract Columns

```bash
cut -d: -f1,3 /etc/passwd     # fields 1 and 3, colon delimiter
cut -c1-10 file.txt            # characters 1-10
```

### tr — Translate Characters

```bash
tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]' < file.txt
tr -d ' ' < file.txt           # delete spaces
tr -s '\n' < file.txt          # squeeze repeated newlines
tr ',' '\t' < data.csv         # replace comma with tab
```

### diff — Compare Files

```bash
diff file1 file2
diff -u file1 file2            # unified format (for patches)
```

### comm — Compare Sorted Files

```bash
comm file1 file2               # 3-column output: file1-only, file2-only, common
comm -12 file1 file2           # only lines common to both
```
codeExample:
sort -t: -k3 -n /etc/passwd | head
ps aux | awk '{print $11}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head
wc -l *.py
cut -d: -f1 /etc/passwd | sort
echo "hello world" | tr 'a-z' 'A-Z'

quiz.question: What does `uniq -c` do?
options: Prefixes each line with its occurrence count
